channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-354] cascading_rw keep running without actually write a thing on RHEL6
item.description: cascading_rw keeps running for an abnormal long time without actually writing, so does metabench
item.summary: cascading_rw keep running without actually write a thing on RHEL6

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment: Reopening as a patch landed to 2.3. will resolve/fixed on version 2.3

Integrated in lustre-dev » x86_64,client,el6,inkernel #340
LU-354 test: Change dev_set_rdonly() check to warning (Revision c65b502fe9feda61c7ad7137f5022c654c6ba501)
LU-354 Change dev_set_rdonly() check to warning (Revision 2bae24e9dcc4765d5d056afdf173febd76f2d1bc)

Result = SUCCESS

not lustre bug.
It turns out that mpi causes this problem, just remove all the mpi options and the tests can pass. so close it.

syslog from mds:
--------------------
Lustre: 3093:0:(quota_master.c:793:close_quota_files()) quota[0] is off already
Lustre: 3093:0:(quota_master.c:793:close_quota_files()) quota[1] is off already

Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: only running test cascading_rw
Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: excepting tests: parallel_grouplock
Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: Using TIMEOUT=20

Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: == parallel-scale test cascading_rw: cascading_rw == 15:52:52 (1306536772)

Would be good to have some sort of a log or a failed test run pointer.

Oleg

Could you please look into this one? It cropped up multiple times during RHEL6 NFS testing

Thanks

Peter
